OLD states unequivocally that no direct or indirect financing or guarantee is offered. NEW introduces franchisor/affiliate financing for corporate-store purchases (up to 100%, 0–12% interest, 12–60 month term, first-lien security) and lease guarantees, with detailed terms, default consequences, waivers, and mandatory personal guarantees — a major expansion of financing rights and obligations.

oldEvidence.quote: We do not offer any direct or indirect financing or financing arrangement, nor will we guaranty your obligations under any note or other obligation.

newEvidence.quote: We do not offer direct financing to franchisees. However, in limited circumstances and in our sole discretion, we or one of our affiliates may provide financing in connection with your purchase of a corporate-owned restaurant sold on an “as-is” basis, or in other limited, one-off circumstances.
If you finance any portion of the purchase price of the corporate-owned restaurant through one of our affiliates, you will also enter into a “Promissory Note and Security Agreement” and a Guaranty...

The annual rate of interest charged will be between 0% and 12% and will depend on the creditworthiness of the franchisee, the amount being financed, and the dollar amount being paid up-front by the franchisee.

rows.summary: No substantive change: both versions state the franchisor does not offer financing or guarantees; NEW omits SBA Registry eligibility language but retains core disclaimer, and the omission appears to be a disclosure scope reduction without contractual effect — however, since OLD’s SBA statement is factual (eligibility status) and NEW removes it without replacement or explanation, this *could* reflect a material change in program participation — yet per instructions, absence of provider/offer language in both means no financing offer exists in either, and removal of administrative eligibility statement alone does not rise to score ≥1 without evidence it reflects an actual policy or status change. No verbatim contradiction or new obligation introduced.
